battlenet_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects the Battle.net Agent's installed games.
battlenet_auto_detection_note: Playlite reads product.db from the Battle.net Agent to detect installed games, enriched with data from aggregate.json when available.
battlenet_linux_description: Battle.net is not supported on Linux.
battlenet_linux_note: Battle.net does not run reliably via Wine, so this integration is Windows-only for now.
battlenet_import_note: Note: Only installed games are detected. Battle.net does not expose total playtime or your full account library.
ea_description: Import your installed EA App games
ea_install_dir_description: Recommended. Select the folder where your EA App games are installed to detect which games are currently installed
ea_info_note: EA does not provide a reliable way to authenticate or list your full game library. Detection relies entirely on the installation folder you configure below, and only installed games will be found.
ea_import_item_owned: ✓ Games installed before, even if now uninstalled (Windows only)
ea_import_note_details: Note: Uninstalled games are imported based on EA App's local install history and may be marked as not installed.
epic_description: Import your Epic Games library: installed games via automatic scan, or your full owned library by connecting your account
epic_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects Epic Games Launcher manifests.
epic_scanner_item_files: The scanner reads .item files containing information about installed games (name, path, executable).
epic_wine_description: On Linux, configure the Wine prefix in the Wine tab to detect the installation.
epic_wine_note_prefix: The manifests directory is resolved automatically from the
epic_wine_note_suffix: No additional configuration is required here.
epic_import_item_owned: ✓ Owned games (purchases and free claims), even if not installed
epic_login_description: Sign in to import your full Epic Games library, including games you own but haven't installed
epic_connected_description: Your Epic Games account is connected. You can disconnect at any time
executable_save_failed: Failed to save game to the database.
executable_dialog_description: Select the main executable to manage the game.
executable_tip: Tip: Recommended files usually include the game icon and a higher internal score.
gog_description: Import your GOG library via account login.
gog_connected_description: Your GOG account is connected. You can disconnect at any time.
gog_login_description: Sign in with your GOG account to import your owned games.
gog_import_note: Once connected, you can import your full GOG library at any time.
gog_import_note_details: Note: Only owned games are imported. Installation status is not detected automatically for GOG.
gog_games_dir_description: Optional: provide the folder where GOG Galaxy stores installed games.
heroic_description: Import games installed via Heroic Games Launcher (Linux and Windows).
heroic_auto_detection_description: Playlite automatically detects Heroic's configuration directory.
heroic_custom_dir_description: Manually provide the configuration directory if automatic detection fails.
heroic_duplicate_warning_prefix: If you import games via Heroic
heroic_duplicate_warning_middle_a: corresponding native launcher (Epic, GOG, etc.), the same
heroic_duplicate_warning_suffix: expected: each entry represents a distinct installation or platform.
legacy_description: Import games acquired via Legacy Games Launcher.
legacy_auto_detection_description: The scanner automatically finds the launcher's state file.
legacy_auto_detection_note_suffix: from Legacy Games Launcher to detect acquired and installed games.
legacy_wine_description: On Linux, configure the Wine prefix in the Wine tab to detect the installation.
legacy_wine_note_suffix: is automatically used to locate the launcher. No additional configuration is required here.
legacy_manual_path_description: If automatic detection fails, point directly to app-state.json.
legacy_import_note: Note: Legacy Games Launcher must be installed, and you must have logged in at least once so the state file exists.
scanner_description: Find and import games installed locally outside official stores.
scanner_directory_description: Select the folder where your games are installed.
scanner_empty_state: Select a folder and click "Start Scan" to begin.
steam_description: Manage how Playlite interacts with your Steam library.
steam_api_credentials_description: Required to import your full library and achievements.
steam_help_title: Where do I get this data?
steam_help_description: You can get your API Key for free on Steam's official site: Steam Web API. To find your SteamID64, you can use tools like STEAMID I/O.
steam_install_dir_description: Location where .acf files and executables are stored.
ubisoft_description: Import installed games and library entries from Ubisoft Connect.
ubisoft_import_note_prefix: Note: Ubisoft Connect must be installed. Detection is automatic via
ubisoft_import_note_suffix: . Library games that are not installed will also be imported as not installed.
wine_description: Configure the Wine prefix to run Windows games on Linux.
wine_linux_only_suffix: to run games built for Windows. On native Windows, this setting has no effect. All configured platforms (Steam, Epic, Heroic, Ubisoft) use the same prefix defined here when launching games via Wine.
wine_prefix_description: Directory containing the Windows environment emulated by Wine (WINEPREFIX).
wine_how_used_description: The prefix is passed as an environment variable when starting games.
wine_command_intro: When launching a Windows game on Linux, Playlite runs:
wine_default_prefix_note: If not configured, Wine uses the default prefix
